module.name: server
module.purpose: Server crate namespace for model/session/task/prompt/step orchestration and server-side plan runtime storage. The binary hosts telemetry on config.server.telemetry_port, Session accepts client and host channels by ChannelEndpoint role, Step emits flat StepDraft model plan events, and the step module's ExecutionHub tracks tool executions keyed by execution signature before completing steps while a parallel RelayHub mirrors it.

childModules.name: model
childModules.purpose: Owns server model backend traits, request/response data, backend errors, and the implemented Deepseek streaming backend. Internal backend implementations expose request directly instead of a send adapter.

childModules.name: plan
childModules.purpose: Owns server-side plan runtime orchestration. Plan delegates StepDraft conversion to Step::from_draft without numeric step-number allocation, exposes run step signatures, and works with PlanHub, PlanRecord, PlanStringify, and PlanError for routing, storage, rendering, and invalid draft reporting.

childModules.name: prompt
childModules.purpose: Defines the server prompt interface, render_template rendering, InitialPrompt, and AnalysisPrompt for execution-output analysis.

childModules.name: session
childModules.purpose: Owns Session startup/routing behavior, accepting client and host channels by ChannelEndpoint role, forwarding Task status/preview and Step events to clients through a matches! filter, routing task, step, execution, relay, and plan events back to the owning Task worker, plus SessionContext snapshots for prompts.

childModules.name: step
childModules.purpose: Owns the server runtime Step type, flat StepDraft to runtime StepKind conversion, task-step routing, internal Initial/Analysis flat StepDraft plan creation, model JSON answer/plan handling, model-prompt rendering, completion handling, and the execution and relay tracking submodules.

childModules.name: task
childModules.purpose: Owns server task runtime and TaskState (session context, a session event sender and a task event sender, plan_hub, an ExecutionHub and RelayHub owning execution and relay tracking, and a StepId-keyed step queue); the worker handles terminal Cancel/Succeed inline, step routing lives on Step, plan events route to PlanHub, and execution/relay routing lives in their hubs.
